title: austrocritonia angulicaulis
intro: Austrocritonia angulicaulis, also known as Quebracho Colorado, is a plant native to the Gran Chaco region in South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues, fever, and inflammation. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. Austrocritonia angulicaulis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever.
